title: Cross-Bank Analytics — TEE + MPC (P-05 pattern)
description: Collaborative fraud detection or credit scoring across multiple financial institutions, using hardware isolation (TEE) to host the MPC computation. Neither institution shares raw transaction data; the TEE hosts the MPC protocol execution and provides hardware-enforced confidentiality alongside cryptographic correctness guarantees.

pet_components.role: hardware isolation of the MPC protocol execution environment — reduces communication overhead and provides hardware-enforced confidentiality of the coordinator computation

parameters.vendor: AMD SEV-SNP via Azure Confidential Computing
parameters.side_channel_mitigations: AMD hardware memory encryption; no SMT on enclave VMs

pet_components.parameter_notes: AMD SEV-SNP chosen over Intel SGX for larger enclave memory limits; trade-off is a different attestation chain and different side-channel posture.

pet_components.role: cryptographic correctness guarantee — participating banks learn only the agreed joint output (e.g. fraud score, credit risk band); neither party learns the other's raw transaction data
pet_components.parameter_notes: SPDZ2k chosen for integer-native arithmetic suited to transaction data. Malicious adversary extension (MAC-based) under evaluation but adds overhead. Three-party threshold with 1-corruption assumption matches the inter-bank trust model.

trust_model.summary: Three participating banks, each holding sensitive transaction records. The TEE coordinator is operated by a neutral cloud infrastructure provider. Each bank is modelled as honest-but-curious — they will follow the protocol but may attempt to learn more from outputs. No single bank is trusted with another's raw data. The MPC protocol guarantees that only the agreed joint output is learned; the TEE provides hardware-enforced confidentiality of the coordinator's computation.

threat_actors.capability: can observe all protocol messages it legitimately receives and attempt to infer the other parties' data
threat_actors.mitigated_by: MPC — SPDZ2k protocol guarantees that a semi-honest party learns only the joint output; TEE provides additional hardware isolation of the coordinator state
threat_actors.residual_risk: Malicious deviation (actively corrupted party) is not covered by the current adversary model; requires MAC-check extension to SPDZ protocol

threat_actors.capability: can observe all VM memory and network traffic outside the TEE boundary
threat_actors.mitigated_by: TEE — AMD SEV-SNP memory encryption isolates enclave from hypervisor
threat_actors.residual_risk: Hardware-level side-channel attacks; AMD SEV-SNP side-channel posture less well-studied than Intel SGX equivalents

threat_actors.actor: external attacker with knowledge of joint output
threat_actors.capability: can attempt to infer individual transaction patterns from released fraud scores or credit bands
threat_actors.mitigated_by: MPC output is a joint aggregate; individual record inference requires knowledge of other parties' inputs
threat_actors.residual_risk: Output granularity may allow partial inference; consider adding DP to released outputs (P-04/P-05 extension) if outputs are fine-grained

out_of_scope_threats: Regulatory non-compliance at individual institution level
Data quality or definition mismatches across institutions
Side-channel attacks at the network layer (timing, traffic analysis)

required_artefacts.artefact: attestation report and enclave measurement (TEE layer)
required_artefacts.notes: Attestation workflow design in progress; measurement pinning policy to be agreed across three banks
Honest-but-curious majority; three-party, one-corruption threshold. Malicious extension documented as future work.
Third-party cryptographic audit scoped for Q4 2026
Communication round benchmarks required before production go/no-go

required_artefacts.artefact: side-channel mitigation declaration for the aggregation enclave

assurance_targets.maturity_justification: Protocol specified and adversary model declared (Stage 2 threshold met). Stage 3 blocked by absence of third-party implementation audit and formal benchmark documentation.

governance_controls.access_control: Three-party joint governance committee; protocol initiation requires agreement from all three participating banks; output release requires joint sign-off
governance_controls.change_control: Protocol version changes require joint governance committee approval; attestation measurement changes require re-approval from all parties

deployment_context.sub_sector: cross-institutional fraud detection and credit risk analytics

applicable_regulations.gap_notes: Joint controller agreement under Art. 26 required between participating banks; in drafting

standards_alignment.notes: MPC and TEE combination consistent with ICO PETs guidance; implementation audit pending

tooling_candidates.name: MP-SPDZ
tooling_candidates.notes: Leading academic-to-production MPC framework; SPDZ2k, semi-honest and malicious variants

tooling_candidates.name: CrypTen
tooling_candidates.notes: PyTorch-based MPC; easier integration with ML pipelines

tooling_candidates.name: MOTION
tooling_candidates.notes: High-performance MPC framework; good for benchmarking

tooling_candidates.name: Gramine (Graphene-SGX successor)
tooling_candidates.notes: Enables running unmodified Linux applications inside Intel SGX or AMD SEV enclaves

residual_risks.risk: Honest-but-curious adversary model does not cover malicious deviation by a participating bank; an actively corrupted party could deviate from the protocol
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Extend to malicious-secure SPDZ protocol with MAC verification; document accepted risk for pilot phase

residual_risks.risk: Third-party implementation audit not yet completed; known-vulnerability classes in MPC implementations not ruled out
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Complete cryptographic audit (scoped Q4 2026) before production deployment

residual_risks.risk: Communication overhead of MPC at production transaction volumes may exceed latency SLAs
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Benchmark at representative transaction volumes; TEE co-location may reduce some latency

residual_risks.risk: No formal output disclosure bound — MPC output granularity may allow partial inference from released scores
residual_risks.mitigation_path: Consider adding DP noise to released outputs post-MPC computation to bound individual-level leakage

change_log.change: Initial card created as YAPS example
change_log.author: Privacy Explorer
